items.rationale: House's highest-conviction 'killer app' — found product-market fit, cheapest way to move a dollar, seen as the on-ramp to bring billions onchain and a national-security answer to CBDCs.

citations.quote: Stablecoins are the "killer app" of crypto, and here's a bunch of great data that shows it.
Stablecoins have found product-market fit
Stablecoin adoption is on the rise as infra developments bring down fees.

items.rationale: Framed as the next wave and the future of consumer crypto — using tokens to coordinate user-owned physical services as the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies.

citations.quote: The future of consumer crypto? DePIN
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.

items.rationale: Treated as a defining convergence — blockchains enforce ownership/identity/authenticity against AI deepfakes, and AI agents are natural crypto-wallet users that dissolve UX friction.

citations.quote: This time is no different: AI needs blockchain-enabled computing.
"AI agents can't have bank accounts, but they can have crypto wallets."

items.rationale: Cryptography wing sees verifiable compute as a new developer primitive and the endgame for scaling/privacy; high long-run conviction but now explicitly tempered on near-term readiness.
items.age_note: Very bullish 2024 (Jolt launch, 'ZK is under hyped'); cooled on near-term deployment by March 2025 — flagged zkVMs as bug-ridden and 'not ready for prime time'

citations.quote: I still think ZK is under hyped.
while SNARKs and zkVMs show immense promise, they're not ready for complex, high-stakes deployments.

items.rationale: Viewed as popular and growing with cheap L2 rails; active recent deal-making (Legend, Universal, Morpho) aimed at making DeFi simple enough for the next 100M users.

citations.quote: DeFi remains popular — and it's growing
leading the $15M seed round in @legendapp to simplify DeFi and help bring the next 100M users onchain.

items.rationale: Programmable IP layer seen as essential coordination infrastructure for the coming flood of AI-generated content; NFTs valued for digital property rights and creator-fan ties.

citations.quote: We want to help Story become the infrastructure for AI systems and creative people to happily co-exist.
NFTs unlock incredible opportunities for creators.

items.rationale: Major scaling upgrades drove fees to sub-penny, finally making infrastructure ready for apps; solving rollup fragmentation (shared sequencing, real-time proofs) is the live frontier.

citations.quote: USDC transfers on Ethereum L2s now cost less than a penny.
Blockchain infra is now ready for apps to flourish

items.rationale: Open protocols like Farcaster cited as a live experiment with growing developer ecosystems and a counterweight to platform control of discourse.

citations.quote: The Decentralized Social experiment is underway, and vibrant developer ecosystems are emerging around open protocols like Farcaster.

items.rationale: The house's most sustained worry — speculation deters serious founders, hands ammunition to regulators, and damages crypto's reputation; consistently framed as gambling, not value creation.
items.age_note: Persistent and reinforced — 'we don't need the casino' (2023) through 'just the next evolution of the gambling industry' (2025); stance unchanged

citations.quote: Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails.
Meme coins are just the next evolution of the gambling industry.

items.rationale: Fast paths to liquidity (short lockups, fixed token allocations, SPAC-like dynamics) incentivize hype-and-dump over building; insiders should be locked up until decentralization is achieved.

citations.quote: Unfettered access to secondary market liquidity is an existential threat to innovation.
4 year/1 year cliff token lockups should be standard.

items.rationale: Newest framework-driven concern — tokens tied to a controlling company carry securities-like risk yet get marketed as decentralized network tokens or memecoins; conflation seen as dangerous.
items.age_note: Crystallized into a formal worry in Q1 2025 with the token-taxonomy work

citations.quote: Company-backed tokens are *not* network tokens.
If there is only one company with actual control of the system... this is not a (decentralized) network token.

items.rationale: Dominant 2024 fear that securities/broker rules were weaponized to bankrupt builders and drive crypto offshore — but the team now sees this receding rapidly.
items.age_note: Top concern through 2024; cooling sharply by Q1 2025 as the SEC dropped its Coinbase case and the IRS broker rule was repealed (70-27)

citations.quote: The weaponization of regulatory uncertainty by the SEC is at an end.
Just like the SEC's attempt to ban DeFi via the "Dealer" rule, the IRS's attempt will not succeed.

items.rationale: Offshore foundations push operational complexity, weaker geopolitical protection, tax risk and value-extraction conflicts; consultants accused of manufacturing urgency to drive US founders offshore unnecessarily.
items.age_note: Sharpened late 2024 into early 2025 as US regulatory outlook improved, making offshoring look unnecessary

citations.quote: we need to kill foreign foundation structures.
some consultants are manufacturing a sense of urgency to push them into foreign foundations as early as possible

items.rationale: A California ruling exposed DAO members to partnership liability for mere participation, making legal wrappers (DUNA) a strategic imperative to avoid untenable tax/liability risk.

citations.quote: any DAO participation (even posting in a forum) could be sufficient to hold DAO members liable

items.rationale: Production SNARKs are seen as riddled with bugs and protected mostly by obscurity; formal verification is years away, making high-stakes deployment premature.

citations.quote: they're not now they're riddled with bugs
Bugs are everywhere, formal verification is nascent

items.rationale: Rising use of trusted execution environments marketed as trustless is seen as a category error that will cause real harm as security assumptions differ fundamentally.

citations.quote: The conflation of trusted execution environments with trustless blockchains is going to lead to a lot of pain.

items.rationale: Research wing flags that proposer-builder separation, exclusive order flow and multi-block MEV create a 'big-get-bigger' dynamic centralizing block building, with cross-chain MEV making it worse.

citations.quote: PBS is reducing centralization in validation, but might create centralization in building

items.rationale: Portfolio companies — even pre-revenue, pre-token startups with ordinary expenses — were losing bank access without justification, treated as a deliberate anti-tech pressure tactic.
items.age_note: Acute concern late 2024; expected to ease with the changing US posture

citations.quote: Most debankings of @a16zcrypto portcos happened to pre-revenue and pre-token companies.

items.rationale: The canonical 'network token' — repeatedly defended as decentralized and not a security, with real fee-based value accrual and expected long-run deflationary dynamics.

citations.quote: ETH is not a security.
long term I think most of us expect eth to be a deflationary currency

items.rationale: Repeatedly cited as the metrics leader — top chain by active addresses, driving DEX volume and mobile-wallet (Phantom) growth; endorsement is data-driven rather than ideological.

citations.quote: The top chain by monthly active addresses: @solana.

items.rationale: Respected as the most secure, decentralized network and digital gold — distinguished from memecoins by genuine economic characteristics, though strategic-reserve hype is downplayed as a priority.

citations.quote: being the native asset of what is arguably the most secure and decentralized network gives it economic characteristics

items.rationale: Held up as a model network token that can capture protocol fees through mechanisms like UniStaker once the fee switch is turned on.

citations.quote: Network tokens like ETH, SOL, and UNI can capture value from their underlying networks through protocol fees.

items.rationale: Treated as ownership of nothing and pure speculation — distinct from network tokens, and emblematic of the casino dynamic the house argues sets the industry back.

citations.quote: Memecoins are the opposite of equity — ownership of nothing versus something.

items.rationale: The recurring cautionary example of a company-backed token — economically a profits interest in a centralized business (FTX), carrying full securities-like risk despite token form.
items.age_note: Used illustratively as the anti-pattern to ETH in the 2025 token-taxonomy framework

citations.quote: secondary transactions of FTT should be subject to securities laws as the instrument was essentially a profits interest in FTX

items.rationale: Standing warning against undercollateralized algorithmic stablecoin designs; the house wants more stablecoin experimentation but explicitly excludes a Terra-style repeat.
items.age_note: Rooted in the 2023 Terraform/Howey commentary; restated as a live caution in Feb 2025

citations.quote: With increasingly obvious product-market fit for stablecoins, I expect more experiments here (no LUNA please).

risk_regime.rationale: As of Q1 2025 the house is broadly and increasingly bullish: scaling has made infrastructure 'ready for apps,' usage metrics (mobile wallets, stablecoin volume, active addresses) are at all-time highs, and the US regulatory tide has turned (SEC dropping cases, IRS broker rule repealed, prospect of bipartisan market-structure legislation). Crucially this is a DISCERNING risk-on: high appetite for 'productive' crypto (stablecoins, DePIN, crypto×AI, decentralized networks with real value accrual) and pronounced risk-OFF on memecoins, unfettered speculation, company-backed tokens and premature/insecure tech (zkVMs, TEEs-as-trustless). The optimism is structural and conviction-driven, not a momentum chase — repeatedly paired with 'just keep building no matter what.'

notes: Recency weighting drove three notable shifts versus the older corpus: (1) the regulatory-enforcement fear that dominated 2024 is explicitly receding by Q1 2025 ('the weaponization of regulatory uncertainty by the SEC is at an end,' 2025-02-21), so it is downgraded from a top structural threat to a fading tactical one. (2) ZK/zkVM enthusiasm, peak-bullish in 2024, was deliberately tempered in March 2025 on security/performance grounds — recorded as conviction-with-caveat rather than reversal. (3) The 2025 'token taxonomy' work (network vs. company-backed tokens; control-based decentralization) is the current organizing lens for nearly all token views and underpins both the ETH-excited and FTT/company-token-concerned entries. The corpus is multi-author: a payments/consumer-adoption voice (stablecoins, metrics), an infrastructure voice (DePIN, ZK, L2 interop), a cryptography-research voice (Jolt/SNARK caution, MEV/PBS), and a legal/policy voice (decentralization, securities framing) — but they are mutually reinforcing rather than conflicting, so a genuine house consensus exists. Strongest unifying thread across all authors and the full 18 months: decentralization-as-control-minimization is the core value proposition, and speculation/the casino is the core internal threat. Conviction scores reflect intensity of stated excitement/concern, not probability of outcomes.
